ArchitectureNotes.objective: Single-file Python CLI with interactive wizard, dual-engine compression, and cross-platform binary builds
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: No web interface, no cloud, no GUI in this repository
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Ghostscript path resolution and error messages vary by platform — must be robust
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: Binary distribution must work without Python installed on target machine

fn-get_deps.PURPOSE: Scan for Ghostscript and pypdf availability

fn-find_pdfs.PURPOSE: Search and sort PDFs in current directory

notes.note-1: Single-file design by choice — easy to distribute as standalone script

contract.purpose: GitHub Actions CI/CD for binary builds and releases
Unit and integration tests for compression modules

fn-test_dependency_detection.PURPOSE: Verify Ghostscript and pypdf detection logic

DF-COMPRESS.NAME: Interactive Compression
DF-COMPRESS.step-2: Fuzzy file search across PDFs in current directory
DF-COMPRESS.step-4: Compress via preferred engine, fallback to secondary
DF-COMPRESS.step-5: Display before/after size and savings percentage
DF-COMPRESS.evidence: Terminal output shows engine used, compression ratio, output file path

DF-DEPENDENCY-CHECK.NAME: Engine Detection
DF-DEPENDENCY-CHECK.step-3: Report status with install instructions for missing engines
DF-DEPENDENCY-CHECK.evidence: Methods output lists each engine as available or missing

DF-BUILD-RELEASE.NAME: Binary Build & Release
DF-BUILD-RELEASE.step-4: Upload artifact and create GitHub Release
DF-BUILD-RELEASE.evidence: GitHub Release page contains 3 binary artifacts

Phase-1.name: Stabilize Core
Phase-1.goal: CLI wizard works end-to-end with both engines
Phase-1.step-1: Compression wizard with fuzzy search, quality selection, dual-engine
Phase-1.step-2: Tests for dependency detection and compression fallbacks

Phase-2.name: Binary Distribution
Phase-2.goal: Prebuilt binaries available for macOS, Linux, Windows
Phase-2.step-1: GitHub Actions builds and publishes release binaries

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only module source files and module-local tests inside the approved write scope.
